Preferred_Parent.Parent_String: mirrors by form of frame [300226131], mirrors by form [300165317], mirrors [300037682], furnishings by form or function [300037342], furnishings (works) [300037336], Furnishings (hierarchy name) [300037335], Furnishings and Equipment (hierarchy name) [300264551], Objects Facet [300264092]

Descriptive_Note.Note_Text: Full-length adjustable mirrors suspended on four-legged frames.
Úsese para espejos regulables de cuerpo entero colgados sobre marcos de cuatro patas.
Wordt gebruikt voor draaibare passpiegels op een vierpotig frame.
可調整傾斜的全身鏡，鏡子外框裝置有四個腳架。

Source.Source_ID: 2000052012/Davidson and Stillinger, American Wing at the MMAt (1985)
2000011151/Fleming and Honour, Penguin Dictionary of the Decorative Arts (1989)
2000034031/Kinckley, Queen Anne and Georgian Looking Glasses (1987)
2000046682/Random House Dictionary of the English Language (1987)

Term_Source.Page: GLOS, Full-length looking-glasses pivoted btwn upright supports, called Psyches in France.

Source.Source_ID: 2000011151/Fleming and Honour, Penguin Dictionary of the Decorative Arts (1989)
